channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年03月19日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] The Download: glass chips and “AI-free” logos
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Future AI chips could be built on glass  Huma

item.title: [未译] The FBI is buying Americans’ location data
item.description: WASHINGTON, DC - MARCH 18: Federal Bureau of Investigation Director Kash Patel testifies during a Senate Intelligence Committee hearing on worldwide threats in the Hart Sena

item.title: [未译] Senegal calls for inquiry after being stripped of AFCON title
item.description: In tonight's edition, Senegal is demanding a corruption investigation, after the Confederation of African Football stripped the Lions of Teranga of their 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title. Also, Nigeri

item.title: [未译] A sufficiently detailed spec is code
item.description: <p><a href="https://lobste.rs/s/nlyezv/sufficiently_detailed_spec_is_code">Comments</a></p>

item.title: [未译] Federal cyber experts called Microsoft's cloud a "pile of shit," approved it anyway
item.description: One Microsoft product was approved despite years of concerns about its security.

item.title: [未译] Micron revenue almost triples, tops estimates as demand for memory soars
item.description: Micron's stock has soared this year while its tech peers have struggled, as the impact of rising memory costs ripples across the industry.

item.title: [未译] International shipping 'chaos' as Strait of Hormuz closure sends ripple effects across the globe
item.description: Around 90% of global goods are transported by sea. And while just approximately 5% of that travels directly through the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's effective closure of the key passage is having a ripple

item.title: [未译] Why Hungary's Viktor Orban is vilifying Ukraine before crucial elections
item.description: Weeks before crucial parliamentary elections, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s campaign has characterised his main rival as a stooge of Kyiv and Brussels. With his party trailing in the polls,

item.title: [未译] US seeks to curb soaring petrol prices
item.description: The Trump administration is taking steps to curb rising energy costs, as it continues to wage war in the Middle East. The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela, and suspending a century-old shipping law

item.title: [未译] Congress considers blowing up internet law
item.description: Internet platforms' liability shield Section 230 faced another round of attack at a Senate Commerce Committee hearing on Wednesday, this time with two distinct undercurre

item.title: [未译] Nothing CEO Carl Pei says smartphone apps will disappear as AI agents take their place
item.description: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says AI agents will eventually replace apps, shifting smartphones toward systems that understand intent and act on a user's behalf.

item.title: [未译] David Sacks’ big Iran warning gets big time ignored
item.description: Sec. Scott Bessent, President Donald Trump, and David Sacks during The White House Digital Assets Summit in Washington, DC, on Friday, March 7, 2025. | Chris Kleponis/CNP/Bl

item.title: [未译] Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
item.description: Nvidia's networking business raked in $11 billion last quarter despite getting significantly less fanfare than chips and gaming.

item.title: [未译] *Recession*, by Tyler Goodspeed
item.description: The subtitle is The Real Reasons Economies Shrink and What To Do About It. Here is from the book’s summary: Contrary to popular perception, recessions are not the inevitable bust that follows an unsustainable boom, and they do not operate like

item.title: [未译] 逛了一天AWE，我突然发现原来这些未来AI黑科技离我们这么近
item.description: 如果说CES展是看未来科技的发展趋势，那么这两天的AWE展就是看AI科技会如何改变咱们的生活，通过一整天的逛展，我确实看到了AI科技已经走入咱们生活的方方面面。下载虎嗅APP，第一时间获取深度独到的商业科技资讯，连接更多创新人群与线下活动

item.title: [未译] Wednesday assorted links
item.description: 1. Congestion pricing for WDC? It is good to see a move away from the selective invocation of economic reasoning, and recognition that some degree of congestion does not justify every tariff. 2. Zimbabwean Uber drivers in Cape Town. 3. Northern Maria

item.title: [未译] Hundreds of Millions of iPhones Can Be Hacked With a New Tool Found in the Wild
item.description: A powerful iPhone-hacking technique known as DarkSword has been discovered in use by Russian hackers. It can take over devices running iOS 18 that simply visit infected websites.

item.title: [未译] Nurturing agentic AI beyond the toddler stage
item.description: Parents of young children face a lot of fears about developmental milestones, from infancy through adulthood. The number of months it takes a baby to learn to talk or walk is often used as a benchmark

item.title: [未译] Sam Altman’s thank-you to coders draws the memes
item.description: Altman expresses gratitude for people who knew how to write their code from scratch. The internet replies with salty jokes.

item.title: [未译] How China is getting everyone on OpenClaw, from gearheads to grandmas
item.description: China's ClawdBot use is soaring as tech giants hold meet-ups to help everyone add the artificial intelligence digital assistant to their devices.

item.title: [未译] Kagi Translate's AI answers the question "What would horny Margaret Thatcher say?"
item.description: Remember when it was fun to play around with LLMs?

item.title: [未译] Musk’s tactic of blaming users for Grok sex images may be foiled by EU law
item.description: Planned EU ban on nudify apps would likely force Musk to make Grok less "spicy."

item.title: [未译] Coal plant forced to stay open due to emergency order isn't even running
item.description: Department of Energy's attempts to prop up coal can look pretty pointless.

item.title: [未译] Amazon doesn’t want the blame for the Post Office going under
item.description: Amazon is going on the defensive after a report from The Wall Street Journal revealed that the ecommerce giant plans to slash shipments with the United States Postal Serv

item.title: [未译] JPMorgan Chase taps Dwyane Wade, Tom Brady for new athlete wealth management push
item.description: JPMorgan's move reflects growing competition among banks and wealth managers to serve athletes, who are increasingly becoming entrepreneurs and investors.

item.title: [未译] Never mind Band-Aids, Neanderthals had antiseptic birch tar
item.description: Our view of Neanderthal life keeps getting more complex and vibrant.

item.title: [未译] Hoto’s powerful PixelDrive electric screwdriver is 25 percent off
item.description: Hoto makes electric screwdrivers for different budgets. If you don’t want to spend much, you can get its most basic model, which we’ve recommended many times. That one co

item.title: [未译] Some simple economics of AI?
item.description: AI lowers the cost of building businesses. But it raises the bar for sustaining advantage. More companies can start. Fewer can dominate. That implies greater dispersion. More volatility. Less structural concentration. A market that rewards adaptabili

item.title: [未译] Cloudflare appeals Piracy Shield fine, hopes to kill Italy's site-blocking law
item.description: Firm says requiring site blocks within 30 minutes breaks core Internet architecture.

item.title: [未译] A private space company has a radical new plan to bag an asteroid
item.description: Company has previously tested its technology on the International Space Station.
